blocksCertification: Low-product-density settings explainer: only 4 distinct verified product photos are genuinely relevant (MOZA R12, Simagic Evo Pro, Fanatec ClubSport DD+, Simucube 2 Sport). Reaching 8 verified images would require forcing irrelevant product cards, which the run rules forbid. Certification should wait on Codex infographics (per-base settings table, Road Effects correction, tune-by-feel flow) and optional real ACC screenshots, not on padded product cards.

Two accuracy dependencies carried in the manifest openQuestions: (a) Road Effects mechanism is disputed across sources (the article states the LOW-on-DD consensus without asserting a mechanism — acceptable, but a human should confirm the framing); (b) per-base Gain is given as ranges reconciling two valid scaling schools. If editorial wants a single authoritative number per base, lock the table to one school before certifying.
Fanatec CSL DD has no verified studio photo, so it is text + Amazon-search link only (needs-download in the image manifest). Not required for this how-to, but flagged.

All prices left as 'check current' (no hard figures quoted) — intentional, but means there is nothing time-stamped to verify at cert time beyond re-confirming the settings guidance still holds post-patch.
